rulings.Case Title: COLE ASHBURY GROUP, LLC VS. COOKIES CREATIVE CONSULTING & PROMOTIONS, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 24, 2026, Line 1, RESPONDENT COOKIES CREATIVE CONSULTING & PROMOTIONS, INC.'s MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. The unopposed motion to withdraw as counsel is granted. The court will sign the order on the appropriate Judicial Council form. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY OVALLE VS. ACQUITY REALITY INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 24, 2026, Line 4, DEFENDANT ACQUITY REALTY INC.'s MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. The unopposed motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted. Defendants Acquity Realty Inc. et al. contend that plaintiff Gregory Ovalle's FEHA claim (sixth cause of action) is unexhausted and his Tameny claim (seventh cause of action) is barred by the two-year limitations period. The court grants leave to amend the sixth cause of action. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. rulings.Case Title: REBECCA BUNTING VS. THE REALREAL, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Compelling Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 24, 2026, Line 6, PLAINTIFF REBECCA BUNTING's Motion To Set Aside Order Compelling Arbitration. Plaintiff has moved under Code of Civil Procedure, 473, subdivision (d), to vacate this court's November 18, 2025 order granting defendant's motion to compel arbitration. The court sua sponte grants reconsideration of the November 18, 2025 order because the court administratively erred in overlooking plaintiff's "Supplemental Declaration" and exhibits, which should have been construed as an opposition to the motion. The court denies plaintiff's motion as moot. The hearing on defendant's motion to compel arbitration and stay this action pending arbitration is reset to April 30, 2026. Plaintiff may file an opposition by no later than nine court days in advance of the continued hearing date. Defendant may elect to proceed on its already-filed reply brief or may file a new reply brief by no later than five court days in advance of the continued hearing date. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: JONIN ABUBACAR CORDOBA VS. POLESTAR AUTOMOTIVE USA INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 24, 2026, Line 7, PLAINTIFF JONIN CORDOBA's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One. Plaintiff Jonin Cordoba's motion to compel further responses to requests for production, numbers 30 and 31, is denied in part and continued in part for further hearing to April 28, 2026. With respect to number 30, Cordoba is entitled to receive documents reflecting other customers' complaints similar to the alleged defects claimed by plaintiff, limited to vehicles purchased in California for the same year, make and model of the subject vehicle. These documents are relevant to Polestar's knowledge of the defects. However, Cordoba's request is too vague for Polestar to ascertain the similarity of other complaints. The request does not define the alleged defects claimed by plaintiff. The parties are ordered to meet and confer within one week. Cordoba must commence by defining a reasonable scope of "similar complaints." If Cordoba fails to do so, Polestar has no further production obligation. With respect to number 31, Polestar asserted objections but made a full representation of compliance: "Subject to and without waiving the foregoing objections, Polestar will comply with this request in full and produce the NHTSA recall documents applicable to the SUBJECT VEHICLE (bates-stamped CORDOBA000001 - CORDOBA000061)." Cordoba does not inform the court what, if anything, is wrong with this representation of compliance. Relief is denied as to this RFP. The court "shall" sanction the party who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel further responses to requests for production of documents unless the court finds that the unsuccessful party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make imposition of the sanction unjust. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2031.310, subd. (h).) No party has requested sanctions. The court gives notice to both parties that it may impose reasonable sanctions upon either party at the continued hearing date if it finds a misuse of the discovery process. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).
